content: Just as human language learners build stronger implicit mastery of vocabulary and writing patterns through repeated deliberate reflection on their work (the educational sense of reflexion), current LLM self-reflection agents can improve their generalization to out-of-distribution reasoning tasks by incorporating spaced, curriculum-aligned reflexion practice modeled after second-language acquisition, rather than only reflecting on single problem attempts. This frames reflexion as not just a one-step error-correction tool for autonomous agents, but a incremental skill-building process adapted from how humans learn.
